ADP-only key. Controls the MemoryLimiter that exerts backpressure when memory exceeds memory_limit. The core agent has no equivalent; it relies on Go GC. Classified as ADP-only / fine as-is in Part 1 review.

Core agent key is enable_payloads.events (dot separator); ADP uses enable_payloads_events (underscore). Feature works in ADP but users setting the documented core agent key will be silently ignored. Tracked in #1366 (config key naming mismatches issue).
Core agent key is enable_payloads.series (dot separator); ADP uses enable_payloads_series (underscore). Same class as enable_payloads_events. Tracked in #1366.
Core agent key is enable_payloads.service_checks (dot separator); ADP uses enable_payloads_service_checks (underscore). Same class as enable_payloads_events. Tracked in #1366.
Core agent key is enable_payloads.sketches (dot separator); ADP uses enable_payloads_sketches (underscore). Same class as enable_payloads_events. Tracked in #1366.

ADP reads env but defaults to 'none' instead of the core agent's ''. Scope is narrower in ADP (only traces/stats encoders). Neither implementation uses env directly on the DogStatsD metrics path, so practical impact for DogStatsD parity is minimal. No action needed.
Implemented in ADP. No divergence noted in the feature doc or meeting notes.
Core agent supports extra_tags to append additional tags to all submitted data. ADP does not support this. Classified as High Priority GA blocker in the audit. Open issue #1332.

ADP-only batching optimization key. Controls how long metrics/stats/traces encoders wait before flushing a partial payload. The core agent has no equivalent. Classified as ADP-only / fine as-is in Part 1 review.

Core agent supports runtime API key refresh without restart. ADP has partial infrastructure (config-side listeners) but the forwarder does not act on updates. Classified as Investigation in the audit. Open issue #1357.

Both ADP and the core agent have this key but defaults diverge: core agent=100, ADP=16. ADP also has no separate high/low priority queues. Classified as Investigation. Open issue #1362.
Core agent allows selecting HTTP protocol version via forwarder_http_protocol. ADP does not support this. Classified as Follow Up in the audit. Open issue #1361.
ADP has no separate low-priority queue — all requests go through one buffer. Classified in the same investigation as forwarder_high_prio_buffer_size. Open issue #1362.

ADP uses concurrency to emulate workers. Possible mapping: forwarder_num_workers -> hyper pool size, forwarder_max_concurrent_requests -> max connections. Needs research. Classified as Investigation. Open issue #1363.

Core agent supports log_to_syslog to direct log output to syslog. ADP has a TODO in logging/mod.rs but no implementation. Classified as High Priority GA blocker. Open issue #1337.

Core agent logs every Nth successful transaction via logging_frequency. ADP has no equivalent mechanism. Classified as Follow Up — design and document ADP's approach, warn on unused setting. Open issue #1380.
ADP-only memory bounding key. The core agent relies on external container limits and Go's GC. Classified as ADP-only / fine as-is.
ADP-only concept. Reduces effective memory_limit to account for untracked allocations. No core agent counterpart. Classified as ADP-only / fine as-is.

Filed as #1353. This key does not exist in the core agent — Saluki invented it as a rename of _blacklist. Covered by #1353.
High Priority issue #1333. Part 3 review: 'definitely a blocker if we aren't already supporting. high priority to check this out.' ADP reads dogstatsd_tags but not the global tags key. Issue is open.
Filed as #1338 (Follow Up). ADP uses data_plane.telemetry_enabled instead of telemetry.enabled. Operator setting telemetry.enabled won't affect ADP; must use ADP's key.
Out of Scope in Part 3: 'Existing GitHub Issue — already tracked. Not a blocker. Existing request timeout covers the gap.' Issue #178 is open.

High Priority issue #1334. Part 3 review: 'This is a blocker.' ADP uses data_plane.dogstatsd.enabled and does not read use_dogstatsd. Customers disabling DogStatsD via the documented setting won't affect ADP.
